step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{C}
**Clinical Reasoning:** Porosity in dental resins typically occurs due to inadequate polymerization, which is more common in thicker sections where heat dissipation and pressure build-up are greater. Thicker parts of the denture experience longer curing times and reduced material flow, leading to voids or porosity when the flask is introduced prematurely. Therefore, porosity is most likely found in the thickest portion of the denture.
